We need to produce a plan achieving goal: (>= (housing location8) 1). So need at least one house at location8. House requires >=1 wood and >=1 stone at location8. Need to produce wood and stone there.

Resources can be obtained via various actions.

Wood: can be produced by saw-wood from timber at a sawmill. Timber can be obtained by fell-timber at a cabin. Cabin requires woodland (location8 is by-coast but not woodland). So cannot build cabin at location8. Need timber elsewhere and transport.

Stone: can be obtained by break-stone at a quarry (mountain). Location4 and 7 are mountains. location4 is mountain and by-coast. location7 is mountain, woodland, by-coast, metalliferous. So we can build quarry at location4 or 7 (requires mountain). Then break-stone to get stone.

We need to get wood to location8. Wood can be produced at sawmill from timber. Sawmill requires >=2 timber at location. So need timber there. Timber can be produced at cabin (requires woodland). Locations with woodland: 0,1,2?, actually location2 not woodland; location3 woodland; location5 woodland; location6 woodland; location7 woodland; location8 not woodland; location9 none. So we can build cabin at any woodland location (0,1,3,5,6,7). Then fell-timber to get timber. Then build sawmill at same location (needs 2 timber). Then saw-wood to get wood.

Alternatively we could transport timber directly to location8 and build sawmill there (needs timber). But building sawmill requires timber, not wood. So we could transport timber to location8, build sawmill, then saw-wood to get wood at location8. That may be simpler: need to get 2 timber to location8, plus later wood for house (need 1 wood). Actually house needs wood and stone. Wood can be from sawmill output. So we need at least 1 wood after saw-wood. So need at least 1 timber to sawmill (but sawmill consumes 1 timber per wood). So need at least 1 timber to produce wood. But building sawmill consumes 2 timber. So total timber needed at location8: 2 for building sawmill + 1 for wood = 3 timber.

We can produce timber at cabin elsewhere, then transport via carts (or train/ship). Need vehicles.

We have potential vehicles vehicle0-6. We can build carts (requires timber). So need timber to build carts. That's circular. Could build train/ship which need iron. Iron requires ironworks, which needs stone and wood. That's also circular.

Simplify: maybe we can avoid building vehicles and just use carts? But need carts to move resources over land. Initially carts-at all locations are 0. So need to build carts at some location using timber. So need timber at that location to build cart. So we need some timber at a location to build cart, then use cart to transport timber elsewhere.

Thus we need to generate timber at a location, then build cart there, then move cart (empty) to other location, load timber, move laden cart, unload.

But building cart consumes 1 timber. So net timber after building cart reduces by 1. So we need extra timbe

Now build mine (requires wood 2): wood becomes 1, timber unchanged.

Now we have timber 10, wood 1.

Now break-stone (stone 1).

Now mine ore 4 times (ore 4).

Now burn coal 10 times: each consumes 1 timber, produce 1 coal. Need 10 timber, we have 10 timber, so after burning: timber 0, coal 10.

Now make iron 4 times: each consumes 1 ore, 2 coal. After 4 times: ore 0, coal 2, iron 4.

Now build ship (consumes iron 4) -> iron 0.

Now move ship (consumes 2 coal) -> ship coal 0.

Now unload wood and stone at location8.

Now build house.

Now we need to ensure that build-ship precondition includes (>= (available iron ?p) 4). At that point we have iron 4 after make-iron. Good.

Now we need to ensure that load action precondition: (is-at ?v ?p) and (> (available ?r ?p) 0) and space >0. Ship is at location7 after build-ship (is-at). Space is 10. So load works.

Now we need to ensure that unload precondition: (is-at ?v ?p) and (> (available ?r ?v) 0). Ship at location8 after move, good.

Now final house building precondition: wood and stone at location8.

Thus plan works.

build-mine: precondition metalliferous and >=2 wood. Wait! Build-mine requires (metalliferous ?p) and (>= (available wood ?p) 2). At location7, we have wood 0 currently. We never produced wood yet. So build-mine fails. That's the error.

We need wood before building mine. Wood can be produced via saw-wood, which needs timber and sawmill. We have sawmill built, timber 14. So we can saw-wood to produce wood. Each saw-wood consumes 1 timber, produces 1 wood. Need at least 2 wood for mine. So we need to do saw-wood at least 2 times before build-mine. Also later we need wood for house (1 wood) but we will transport wood from location7 to location8 via ship, so we need wood at location7 to load onto ship. So we need at least 1 extra wood for house. So total wood needed at location7 before loading: 1 (for house) + 2 (for mine) =3 wood. So we need to saw-wood 3 times.

But also building mine consumes 2 wood, leaving wood 1 for house. Good.

Thus modify plan: after building sawmill, do saw-wood 3 times (instead of 3 earlier we had 3 but we placed before quarry etc). Need to ensure order: saw-wood before build-mine.

Also build-ironworks has no resource preconditions, fine.

Now after building mine, we need wood for house already present (1). Good.

Now break-stone: need quarry built (we have). No resource preconditions.

Now mine-ore 4 times: need mine built (we have). Good.

Now burn-coal: need coal stack built (we have) and timber >=1 each time. We have timber after saw-wood: started 16, used 2 for sawmill, 1 for coal stack, 3 for saw-wood = 16-2-1-3=10 timber left. Good for 10 burns.
